description:
Use this tool to break up design tasks to multiple designer agents to work in parallel. Always create one less designer agent then needed, since the existing agent session will accomplish the last remaining part of the task.

- Use this tool when the task involves creating design with
	- multiple sections (like in websites),
	- multiple app screens,
	- multiple websites, or variations of the same website,
	- variations of some design,
	- anytime there is opportunity to split the work up between designer agents.
- When creating large designs ALWAYS use this tool to break up your main tasks into subtasks for designer agents that can work in parallel and achieve the main task much faster and better.
- IMPORTANT: When you are splitting up related sections, for example sections of a website, make sure to put the separate section nodes into a parent node first.
- Designer agents do not inherit guidelines from the parent agent. If you have already picked a guide or a guideline style, include their names and params in each agent's prompt so the agent can fetch them itself.
- When you know you will spawn designer agents, make sure to create placeholder nodes for them where they can work in. Then pass the node ID of the container node where they should work in as argument in the config array entries.
- The prompt should be brief and should contain a short summary of what the agent needs to design.
- IMPORTANT: Make sure the designer agents prompts describe the designs consistently, so if matching styles are needed, they will all look the same based on the prompt.
- Designer agents can read the design variables in the document, so DO NOT include them into the prompt.
- IMPORTANT: Design agents can come up with the the design layout themselves, it's important to NOT include any layout information, sizes, colors or variable names in the prompt.
- Create maximum 8-10 designer agents at once.
- DO NOT put placeholder on the nodes the agents will work in and also NOT on the parent node if there is any.

## Examples

Main prompt: "create an iOS app for a bank with 4 screens: a sign in/sign up screen, an overview, a transfer and a profile screen."

Thinking: "After checking the editor state, picking the 'Mobile App' guide and creating 4 placeholder nodes for the screens on the canvas, I'm going to use the 'spawn_agents' tool to split the work up between 3 newly spawned designer agents and this session creating each creating one of the screens."

Tool call:
{
	"config": [
		{
			"prompt": "Implement the sign up screen for an iOS bank app. Use the 'Mobile App' guide",
			"containerNodes": ["cde35Sa"]
		},
		{
			"prompt": "Implement the overview app screen for an iOS bank app. Use the 'Mobile App' guide,
			"containerNodes": ["dg3tX2"]
		},
		{
			"prompt": "Implement the transfer app screen for an iOS bank app. Use the 'Mobile App' guide,
			"containerNodes": ["sh3Z1x"]
		}
	]
}

And now I will implement the last part, the profile screen for an iOS bank app.

config.description: The required config for the extra agents that will be spawned and executed in parallel alongside the current agent.

containerNodes.description: The valid node IDs in the document in which the designer agent should accomplish the given task. Make sure to always specify at least one node ID to work in.

prompt.description: The prompt for the designer agent to run.

filePath.description: An optional file path to access a .pen file.

name: spawn_agents
